paper_id: Bastos et al. - 2018 - Using Semantic Documentation to Support Software Project Management

evidence_quotes.quote: Then, taking SPMO as a basis, we developed the Infrastructure for Managing Semantic Documents for Software Project Management (IMSD-SPM), an extension of IMSD with specific features to support project management aspects.

Briefly, here, SPMO is used to assign semantics to documents and spreadsheets content, and also to help develop features to support software project management using a semantic documentation approach.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3 The Software Project Management Ontology (SPMO)

evidence_quotes.quote: To address R1 to R6, we explored SPMO conceptualization for developing domain-specific features that were implemented to support scope, time and costs management activities.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 4.2 Supporting Software Project Scope, Time and Costs Management

evidence_quotes.quote: For implementing SPMO-OWL, we used Protégé, and the resulting OWL file (spmo.owl) was used to annotate the templates of documents and spreadsheets.

goals.justification: The ontology was actively used as the basis to develop and implement the IMSD-SPM tool's domain-specific features and to annotate document/spreadsheet templates. Signal patterns 'used to create', 'implemented', 'generated artifact' apply; this is concrete artifact generation, not future work.

evidence_quotes.quote: (iv) annotating project management related resources in a semantic documentation approach.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3 The Software Project Management Ontology (SPMO)

evidence_quotes.quote: This ontology is used to annotate documents and spreadsheets and to develop features to support scope, time and cost management activities.

SPMO can serve as a reference model for semantic interoperability initiatives and also can provide a common vocabulary and conceptualization for people talk about the software project management domain.

goals.justification: SPMO is explicitly framed as a reference model used to annotate documents/spreadsheets that store project knowledge for later retrieval and asynchronous reuse. Signal patterns 'documentation', 'reference', 'knowledge management', 'repository' apply.

evidence_quotes.quote: The purpose of SPMO is to establish a common conceptualization about the software project management domain, initially focusing on scope, time and costs planning and monitoring.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3 The Software Project Management Ontology (SPMO)

evidence_quotes.quote: SPMO is intended to be used as a reference model for: (i) supporting human learning on the software project management domain; (ii) integrating project management supporting tools; (iii) structuring and representing knowledge related to software project management; and (iv) annotating project management related resources in a semantic documentation approach.
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3 The Software Project Management Ontology (SPMO)

goals.justification: Establishing a common conceptualization and structuring/representing knowledge about the domain matches the 'conceptualize', 'shared understanding' signals for domain understanding.

evidence_quotes.quote: SPMO can serve as a reference model for semantic interoperability initiatives and also can provide a common vocabulary and conceptualization for people talk about the software project management domain.

uncertain_goals.reason: The mention of 'people talk about' suggests communication use, but it is generic and not clearly framed as synchronous discussion/meetings between distinct stakeholder groups; evidence is too thin to code as present.

evidence_quotes.quote: SPMO is intended to be used as a reference model for: (i) supporting human learning on the software project management domain;
evidence_quotes.section: Section 3 The Software Project Management Ontology (SPMO)

uncertain_goals.reason: The 'learning' here is explicitly about the project management DOMAIN, not about learning modeling/methods/notation; per disambiguation rule this belongs under Understanding, so Learning is not appropriately coded.
